zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William de Burgh, 1st Baron Burgh於1295年出生在愛爾蘭康瑙特時，他的父親Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster36歲，母親Margaret Guines36歲。他於大約1320年與Lady Eleanor Cowley在英國結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1337年在英國奧可福爾可拉利享年42歲，埋葬在英國。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir William de Burgh, 1st Baron Burgh于1295年出生在爱尔兰康瑙特时，他的父亲Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster36岁，母亲Margaret Guines36岁。他于大约1320年与Lady Eleanor Cowley在英国结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1337年在英国奧可福爾可拉利享年42岁，埋葬在英国。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Burgh,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from one or other of the ten English places named Burgh in northern England and East Anglia, or the six named Brough in Derbyshire, Nottinghamshire, Westmorland, and Yorkshire. All these are from Old English burg, burh, Middle English burgh ‘massive earthwork, fortification; manor, borough’. Compare Bury . The placename Burgh is pronounced as borough. The surname Brough arose equally from places now called Burgh and Brough, and is the more common form of the surname in modern Britain, where it is pronounced bruff or broof (/brʊf/). The Scottish surname was probably brought in from Yorkshire. For the surname in Ireland see Burke .

variants.name: Burke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Kieran, Siobhan, Aileen, Brennan, Brigid, Donovan, Liam, Murphy, Ulick, Aidan, Dermot.

English and Irish (of Anglo-Norman origin): habitational name from Burgh (Castle) in Suffolk, England. Burk represents an Anglo-Norman pronunciation of Old English burg, Middle English burgh ‘fortification’ (see Burgh ).

variants.name: Bury
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from Bury in Lancashire (now part of Greater Manchester), or from some other similarly named place. The placename comes from the dative case, byrig, of Old English burh ‘fortified place’.

Walloon and French: habitational name from any of several places so named, in Wallonia (Belgium) and Oise (France). The placename is from Buriacum, the name of a Gallo-Roman estate, composed of the personal name Burius + the locative suffix -acum. This surname is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium.

variants.name: Brough
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from any of various English places called Brough or Burgh. See   Burgh  .
Scottish: habitational name from Brough in Harray (Orkney).
Americanized form of German   Brauch   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Burrow
meanings: topographic name for someone who lived by a castle or manor house, or in a town, especially a chartered town or borough (Middle English burgh, Old English burg ‘fortified place, borough, manor’), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named because it was a fortified town or near an ancient fort, such as Burrow (Leicestershire, Lancashire), Burrough Green (Cambridgeshire), Brough (numerous counties), and Peterborough (Northamptonshire); see also Burgh .

topographic name for someone who lived on a hill or near a mound (Middle English bergh, Old English beorg), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named, such as Burrow (Devon, Somerset). Middle English bergh became throughly confused with burgh in 1 above. Compare Burrough .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William de Burgh, 1st Baron Burgh was born in 1295, in Connaught, Ireland, his father, Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster, was 36 and his mother, Margaret Guines, was 36. He married Lady Eleanor Cowley about 1320, in England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. He died in 1337, in Crawley, Oxfordshire, England, at the age of 42, and was buried in England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William de Burgh, 1st Baron Burgh est né en 1295, à Connaught, Irlande, son père, Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Margaret Guines, avait 36 ans. Il a épousé Lady Eleanor Cowley environ 1320, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en 1337, à Crawley, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 42 ans, et a été enterré à Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir William de Burgh, 1st Baron Burgh 1295 in Connaught, Irland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster 36 und seine Mutter Margaret Guines 36. Er hat um 1320 in England Lady Eleanor Cowley geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb 1337 im Alter von 42 Jahren in Crawley, Oxfordshire, England und wurde in England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William de Burgh, 1st Baron Burgh è nato il nel 1295 nel Connaught, Irlanda, il suo padre, Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster, aveva 36 anni e il suo madre, Margaret Guines, aveva 36 anni. Lui sposato Lady Eleanor Cowley circa 1320, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1337, nel Crawley, Oxfordshire, England, all'età di 42 anni, e fu sepolto a England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir William de Burgh, 1st Baron Burghが1295年にアイルランドコナウトで生まれた時，父Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulsterは36歳，母Margaret Guinesは36歳であった。約1320年にイギリスでLady Eleanor Cowleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1337年にイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアクラウリーで42歳で死去し，イギリスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William de Burgh, 1st Baron Burgh(이)가 1295년 아일랜드 콘오그h트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster(은)는 36세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Guines(은)는 36세였다. 그는 대략 1320년 영국에서 Lady Eleanor Cowley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1337년 영국 스퍼드실르 크롤리에서 42세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William de Burgh, 1st Baron Burgh nasceu em 1295, em Connaught, Irlanda, seu pai, Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster, tinha 36 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Guines, tinha 36 anos. Ele casou-se com Lady Eleanor Cowley aproximadamente 1320, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1337, em Crawley, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, com 42 anos, e foi sepultado em Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William de Burgh, 1st Baron Burgh родился в 1295 г. в местности коннот, Ирландия, его отцу по имени Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Guines, 36. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Eleanor Cowley около 1320 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер в 1337 г. в местности кравли, оксфордшир, Англия в возрасте 42 лет и был похоронен в местности Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir William de Burgh, 1st Baron Burgh nació en 1295, en Connaught, Irlanda, su papá, Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster, tenía 36 años y su mamá, Margaret Guines, tenía 36 años. Se casó con Lady Eleanor Cowley aproximadamente 1320, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1337, en Crawley, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 42 años, y fue sepultado en Inglaterra.
